caption: Figure 1: Interpretation process for B4 (left) and B234 (right) on a CUB-200 red-bellied woodpecker. Red and blue indicate higher and lower feature importance. Representative patches are the closest training examples to each prototype; border colors match the explanation map segments.
description2: The figure illustrates the interpretation process for two bird species, B4 and B234, on a CUB-200 red-bellied woodpecker, with representative patches and color-coded feature importance.
description3: Two-panel plot displays input images, explanation maps, and feature attribution heatmaps for B4 and B234, alongside representative patches of each cluster.
questions: How do explanations of red-bellied woodpecker features differ between the two presented examples?
What visual features are considered most and least important in these red-bellied woodpecker interpretations?

caption: Figure 2: UMAP projection of training and test (lower opacity) embeddings from the twenty sparrow classes of CUB-200. Left: classifier prototypes cj as crosses and rescaled to class norm as triangles. Right: KMEx prototypes.
description2: The figure is a UMAP projection of training and test embeddings from 20 sparrow classes in CUB-200, with classifier prototypes as crosses and triangles on the left, and KMEx prototypes on the right.
description3: Two-panel UMAP projections display sparrow class embeddings, with the left panel showing classifier prototypes as crosses and triangles, and the right panel showing KMEx prototypes as squares.
questions: How do classifier prototypes and KMEx prototypes represent the sparrow classes?
What visual distinction is made between training and test embeddings?
